intro: Oplismenus muricatus, commonly known as thyme leaved sparrow's grass, is a type of perennial grass native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Africa and Asia. The plant is rich in health benefits, particularly for its expectorant properties, which help to relieve respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The bioactive constituents of O. muricatus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with O. muricatus include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are traditionally used to treat respiratory problems and other health conditions.
